name: Stars and Stripes Celebration Cake
recipeIngredient: 2 packages yellow cake mix (18 1/4 oz each)
2 pints strawberries, fresh, washed and dried and hulled
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 350 degrees F (175 degrees C) one hour before starting.
2. Grease two 15 1/2 x 10 1/2 x 1 inch jelly-roll pans with butter; line each pan with wax paper and grease the paper.
3. Prepare cake batter according to package directions, working with one package at a time. Spread batter evenly in prepared pans and bake for 15 to 20 minutes until a wooden toothpick inserted into the center comes out clean. Invert cake layers onto wire racks; peel off paper and let cool completely.
4. Toss banana slices with lemon juice in a medium bowl. Drain excess juice and set bananas aside.
5. Slice about 24 strawberries lengthwise into halves, reserving for topping the cake. Coarsely chop remaining strawberries and set aside.
6. In a large bowl fitted with an electric mixer, beat heavy cream until soft peaks form. Gradually beat in confectioners sugar until stiff peaks form. Divide whipped cream evenly between two bowls; fold strawberry puree into one bowl and blueberries into the other.
7. Set both creams aside.
8. Place one cake layer on a serving platter and tuck strips of wax paper under the cake to keep the platter clean. Spread strawberry cream over the top of the first cake layer using a spatula, then place the second layer on top and spread the remaining cream over the top and sides of the cake.
9. Carefully remove the wax paper strips from the cake.
10. Using a sharp knife, mark a 7 1/2 x 5 inch rectangle in the top left corner of the cake. Arrange blueberries in even rows within the rectangle to form stars of the flag. In alternating rows above and below the star formation, arrange banana slices and strawberry halves, overlapping as necessary.
11. Chill the cake for up to 2 hours before serving. Cut into 2 x 1 1/2 inch pieces.
